dataset: Loghub SSH.log (SSH brute force dataset from Zenodo/Loghub)

pi_items.source: Task description specifies outputlookup threat_intel.csv. Live testing confirmed outputlookup creates CSV at /opt/splunk/etc/apps/search/lookups/threat_intel.csv with correct header (src_ip, attempt_count, threat_level) and 1008 rows.
Task description and verifier.py both reference 'threat_intel_lookup'. export_result.sh searches for this exact name (normalized) in /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups.
Task description and export_result.sh both search for 'threat_auto_enrich' in props/lookups API endpoint and btool output.
Task description specifies this exact name. verifier.py normalizes to 'threat_intelligence_monitor' for matching. export_result.sh searches for this name in /servicesNS/-/-/data/ui/views.
Task description specifies this exact name. verifier.py normalizes to 'critical_threat_activity'. export_result.sh searches for this in /servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches.

Live testing confirmed: index 'security_logs' exists with 655,721 events. SSH brute force data from Loghub SSH.log (655,147 events) + live /var/log/auth.log monitor (~585 events). Created by setup_splunk.sh post_start hook.

Live testing confirmed: SSH.log data ingested as sourcetype 'linux_secure'. Verified via REST API: search index=security_logs | stats count by sourcetype shows all 655,147 SSH.log events as linux_secure.
Task description specifies 'every 15 minutes (cron: */15 * * * *)'. verifier.py checks for '*/15' substring in cron_schedule field.
Task description specifies 'at least three panels'. verifier.py awards full 15 pts for panel_count >= 3, partial for 2 or 1.
Task description specifies exact threat levels. Live testing confirmed classification produces: 35 critical, 47 high, 110 medium, 816 low IPs out of 1,008 unique attacking IPs.

Task description specifies: critical (>500), high (>100), medium (>20), low (otherwise). Live testing confirmed these thresholds produce meaningful distribution. Top IP 59.63.188.30 has 28,766 attempts (critical). Threshold of 500 yields 35 critical IPs.
install_splunk.sh user-seed.conf sets USERNAME=admin. All REST API calls verified with admin credentials. Task description states admin/SplunkAdmin1!.
install_splunk.sh user-seed.conf sets PASSWORD=SplunkAdmin1!. All REST API calls verified with these credentials. task_utils.sh defines SPLUNK_PASS=SplunkAdmin1!.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires building a threat intelligence enrichment pipeline in Splunk Enterprise 9.4.0. The security_logs index contains 655,721 events from the Loghub SSH.log dataset (real SSH brute force attacks against host LabSZ) ingested as sourcetype linux_secure. Field extractions for src_ip and user are pre-configured in props.conf (setup_task.sh creates them). The SPL query 'index=security_logs sourcetype=linux_secure "Failed password" | stats count as attempt_count by src_ip | eval threat_level=case(attempt_count>500,"critical",attempt_count>100,"high",attempt_count>20,"medium",1==1,"low")' produces 1,008 unique IPs: 35 critical, 47 high, 110 medium, 816 low. The top attacking IP is 59.63.188.30 (28,766 attempts). The outputlookup command writes to /opt/splunk/etc/apps/search/lookups/threat_intel.csv. The lookup definition and automatic lookup must be created via Settings > Lookups UI. The dashboard must be named Threat_Intelligence_Monitor with 3+ panels referencing threat_level and using timechart/stats. The alert must be named Critical_Threat_Activity with cron */15 * * * *. Splunk credentials: admin / SplunkAdmin1!. All verification is done via REST API at https://localhost:8089.
